meta_title: For Founders: Antigravity project template to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Antigravity Template to Ship Faster
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on your MVP. Use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to automate development and launch your SaaS product in record time.
content.answer_block: The 'Antigravity' project template solves the 'founder's stall' by enforcing a modular, feature-first architecture. By pairing this template with Claude Code, you offload the heavy lifting of boilerplate and infrastructure, allowing you to focus exclusively on business logic and revenue-generating features rather than getting trapped in configuration hell.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS startups is littered with half-baked repositories and 'perfected' architectures that never saw the light of day. You’ve been there: the initial spark of an idea fades into the crushing weight of dependency management, deployment pipelines, and the slow creep of feature fatigue. It’s not a lack of vision; it’s a lack of momentum.

Today, we kill the stagnation. By leveraging the Antigravity project template, we bypass the friction of manual scaffolding and move straight into the execution phase. Paired with Claude Code, you aren't just coding—you are orchestrating an automated development engine designed to get your MVP into the hands of paying users before your motivation wanes.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Establish the foundation by cloning the boilerplate that handles authentication, database connections, and standard API routes out of the box.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new SaaS project using the Antigravity standard stack. Ensure the folder structure is optimized for rapid feature iteration and modular growth.

steps.title: Define the Core Business Logic
steps.description: Translate your unique value proposition into the system. Focus on the single feature that solves your user's primary pain point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the core business requirement: [INSERT YOUR IDEA]. Generate the necessary data schemas and API endpoints to handle this specific function.

steps.title: Automate Database Migrations
steps.description: Avoid manual schema management. Use the template's built-in migration tools to sync your local environment with your staging database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my current schema changes and create the migration scripts. Ensure the database remains compatible with existing user authentication flows.

steps.title: Integrate Monetization Hooks
steps.description: Revenue isn't an afterthought; it's the heartbeat of your project. Connect your payment gateway to the template's pre-built billing module.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate Stripe billing webhooks into the user subscription model. Configure the system to restrict access to premium features based on payment status.

steps.title: Execute the Final Deployment
steps.description: Move from local development to a live environment. Use the template's CI/CD hooks to push your code to your hosting provider.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the deployment pipeline for production readiness. Verify all environment variables and secrets are handled securely.

pro_tips: Never build an admin panel from scratch; use the Antigravity auto-generated dashboard to save 40+ hours of UI work.
Treat your feature set like a lean startup: if a feature doesn't directly contribute to ARR, delete the code.
Use Claude Code's 'review' command periodically to catch technical debt before it becomes an architectural bottleneck.
Always wrap external API calls in a circuit breaker pattern to prevent your entire application from crashing during downtime.
Keep your repository size small by using separate micro-services for heavy assets like media processing.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, spend 80% of your time on customer discovery and 20% on optimizing the onboarding flow based on actual user behavior data.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for yourself and start building for your market. The tools are ready, the framework is set, and the path to revenue is clear. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
